key_insight: A linearly implicit mass-lumped finite element projection scheme can preserve the sphere constraint exactly at nodes while still delivering unconditional stability and optimal convergence for harmonic map flow.
engineering_relevance: This gives a robust, low-cost FEM strategy for constrained nonlinear evolution problems where exact nodal feasibility and stable long-time simulation matter.
method: Predict with a linearly implicit, mass-lumped finite element step derived from a cross-product formulation.
Project the auxiliary solution nodally onto the unit sphere to enforce the constraint exactly at mesh nodes.
Use discrete inner products and tensor-product meshes to obtain contraction in L2 and nonexpansive energy decay.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports provable optimal error rates O(Δt+h^2) in L∞(0,T;L2) and O(Δt+h) in L2(0,T;H1), plus numerical experiments confirming the convergence and discrete energy decay.
watch_item: The discrete energy nonexpansiveness is guaranteed on Cartesian rectangular and cuboidal tensor-product meshes, so the strongest stability result may not directly extend to arbitrary meshes.
paper_title: Unconditional Optimal Error Estimates and Energy Stability for a Linearly Implicit Mass-Lumped Projection Finite Element Method for the Harmonic Map Flow
